name: dspace-mcp
description: Read-only MCP server for DSpace 7+ repositories
long_description:
Searches and reads any DSpace 7+ repository over its REST API: discovery search, items by UUID / handle / DOI, communities and collections, bitstream listings, faceted counts, and text extraction from PDF, Word, OpenDocument and Office XML files.

Read-only by construction: it can send no request that would modify the repository. By default it queries anonymously and sees exactly what the public sees. Optionally give it a DSpace account and it will also read what that account can see — embargoed items, restricted collections and closed files — and can then tell you which files the public cannot reach.

Point it at your own instance with the base URL below (the `/server` endpoint, not the user-facing site).

base_url.title: DSpace base URL
base_url.description: REST API root of your repository, e.g. https://demo.dspace.org/server — the /server endpoint, not the public web UI. Required: the server refuses to start without it rather than guess and hand you someone else's data.

max_results.title: Max results per tool call
max_results.description: Hard cap on how many objects any single tool may return.

extract_max_mb.title: Text extraction size limit (MB)
extract_max_mb.description: Refuse to download bitstreams larger than this when extracting text (PDF, Word, OpenDocument, Office XML).

username.title: Username (optional)
username.description: Leave empty to query anonymously, like any visitor. Fill both this and the password to also read material only your account can see: embargoed items, restricted collections and closed files. Use an account with the least privilege that covers what you need — the server reads everything that account is allowed to read. It still cannot modify anything.

password.title: Password (optional)
password.description: Password for the account above. Required if you filled in a username, ignored otherwise. Only password login is supported: repositories that authenticate solely through ORCID or Shibboleth cannot be used this way, and the server will say so instead of failing silently.
